chapter_title.ar: باب افْتِتَاحِ الصَّلَاةِ وَ الْحَدِّ فِي التَّكْبِيرِ وَ مَا يُقَالُ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 20 – Commencing the Salāt and the limit regarding the exclamations of Takbīr, and what is to be said during that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/98)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/98)
text: أَدْنَى مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنَ التَّكْبِيرِ فِي التَّوَجُّهِ تَكْبِيرَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ وَ ثَلَاثُ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ أَحْسَنُ وَ سَبْعٌ أَفْضَلُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Zurara who said,
‘The lowest of what would suffice from the exclamation of Takbīr during the direction, is one Takbīr; and three Takbīrs are better, and seven would be superior’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is a narration from him (narrator of previous Hadith) by his father Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The least for Takbir (Allah is great beyond description) facing (al-Qiblah (al-Ka‘bah)) is to say it once. Three times is good and seven times is better.”’
